title: Post-implementation review + SYSTEM EVALUATION #2 — Clotho slice 5
note: ADVISORY evaluation anchored to commit-verified facts.
run: clotho slice 5 (delivery git:321473a; agent branches merged at 723703a/3578efe/18c57dd; PR #126)
what_was_built: query.mjs (524) + test-query.mjs (686, 80 units); weave.mjs (697) + test-weave.mjs (778, 106 assertions) + additive orchestrator inventory + closure extension; test-advisory.mjs (946, 72 units); R3 shared manifest validator in util.mjs (+103, closure-safe). coverage-schema + d35/d34/d10 flipped NORMATIVE-CURRENT.
verification_outcome: clotho npm test 13/13; FIRST REAL-REPOSITORY WEAVE PUBLISHED (3892 edges, 4.4MB signed ledger, five weavers executed, publication 'published', 0 fatal warnings); verify-contracts 203/203; build-gate regression green; entry ritual 13/13 GRANTED before work
what_worked: the Daedalus-matured three-agent decomposition delivered ~2,900 new lines in one parallel window, merged conflict-free by one-writer-per-file — the workshop's R1/R3 resolutions were followed exactly and cost nothing at merge
agents adopted the session's mutation-testing discipline UNPROMPTED: both Q and W falsified their own implementations to prove their oracles discriminate before reporting green
test-all's fail-closed unlisted-test guard forced clean incremental integration commits (one merge + wiring per agent), keeping the branch green at every push
the merge-first worktree instruction closed the isolation-base gap (worktrees base on origin/main, which lacks branch work)
defects_found_during: agent-W's first commit carried a raw NUL byte in a test fixture (git treated the file as binary) — the agent caught and fixed it itself (\u0000 escape) before reporting

SPEC-SHAPE MISMATCH (in-envelope, recorded for future amendment): frozen v15 names the warning shape {weaver, code, path, detail} but the accepted 4a/4b weavers emit {weaver, message}; the driver bridges both shapes for attribution and fatal-code enforcement. Harmonization is an amendment candidate (Daedalus -> authz), NOT an implementation liberty
integration_contracts_recorded: currentDocs is a Map<docAddressKey, text_sha256|null> (null = deleted/ambiguous) — the Task 6/7 flagship and any doc-drift caller must construct exactly this shape (agent-Q)
kind->producer map: git=introduced-by, code=depends-on, test=verified-by, doc=documented-in, ledger=motivated-by/evidenced-by/discharges; supersedes has no producing weaver (agent-Q)

redundancy: none introduced: advisory scanner, closure derivation, and the driver's D34 re-derivation all consume the ONE shared classifier/resolver in weavers/util.mjs — proven by a dedicated unit, three consumers zero forks

driver-local helpers (union-closure derivation, canonical comparators, count-shaped-field detection, D19 equality checker) are deliberately driver-owned, not util material — do not 'promote' them without a second consumer

process_dependencies.slice_6_needs: 5's driver + query surface (the flagship acceptance runs a real weave and queries it) — sequential
process_dependencies.slice_7_needs: 6's flagship run (reproduction re-derives it from committed bytes) — sequential
process_dependencies.conclusion: slice-level sequencing unchanged; slice 6 is a single-deliverable slice (flagship test) with limited internal parallelism; slice 7 has TWO independent deliverables (reproduction evidence; documentation/README render) — parallelizable

concurrency_opportunities_via_merkle_dag: slice 7: reproduction-evidence agent and documentation-render agent are disjoint writers (docs/runs/clotho-self-weave/ vs clotho/memory/README.md + renderer) — the slice-5 pattern applies directly
slice 6: keep single-writer; the flagship test's value is the INTEGRATED end-to-end path, which parallelism would only obscure

optimizations_for_future_runs.optimization: warning-shape harmonization ({weaver,code,path,detail} vs {weaver,message}) via a small Daedalus delta when the weavers are next touched — until then the driver's dual-shape bridge is the recorded behavior
optimizations_for_future_runs.lands_in: future amendment candidate; recorded here and in the driver's comments

optimizations_for_future_runs.optimization: slice 7: two parallel disjoint-writer agents (reproduction; renderer) per the slice-5 pattern
agent worktrees base on origin/main — keep the merge-first instruction in every agent prompt until isolation supports basing on the current branch
